q1.question: Does the butterfly have a tail on the hindwing?
q1.hint: Examine the posterior margin of the hindwing for filiform or stub-like projections.

q2.question: What is the tail type?
q2.hint: Compare the length and thickness of the tail projection relative to the wing width.

q3.question: What is the dominant upperside forewing colour?
q3.hint: View the dorsal surface under natural light. Angle of view affects iridescence.

q4.question: Is there a submarginal spot row on the hindwing underside?
q4.hint: Examine the ventral (underside) surface of the hindwing near the margin.

r_centaurus.note: Look for 3 metallic bands on the hindwing underside. The long filiform tail is diagnostic in tailed forms.

r_aedias.note: Small stub tail; underside with pale streaking. Compare with A. centaurus which has more prominent banding.

r_aurea.note: Brilliant blue-purple upperside with no tail. One of the more commonly sighted tailless oak blues.

r_eumolphus.note: Distinctive green iridescence on upperside. No tail. The underside shows characteristic dark streaking.
